questions.question: What is the probability distribution of the number of successes in n independent trials, where each trial has a probability p of success?
questions.explanation: The Binomial distribution is used to model the number of successes in a fixed number of independent trials, where each trial has a constant probability of success.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting exactly k successes in n trials, where the probability of success on each trial is p?
questions.explanation: The Binomial probability formula is P(k) = C(n,k)p^k(1-p)^(n-k), where C(n,k) is the number of combinations of n items taken k at a time.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Binomial distribution?
questions.explanation: The Binomial distribution is skewed to the right, especially when p is small.

questions.question: What is the mean of the Binomial distribution?
questions.explanation: The mean of the Binomial distribution is np, where n is the number of trials and p is the probability of success on each trial.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a parameter of the Binomial distribution?
questions.explanation: While k is the number of successes, it is not a parameter of the Binomial distribution. Instead, it is a random variable.
